title: Michelle Lange
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Michelle Lange
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

biography: Dr. Lange received her B.A. from the College of William & Mary, and her M.A. and Psy.D. in Clinical Psychology from the University of Hartford. She is a Licensed Clinical Psychologist, and has been teaching in CNU's Department of Psychology since 2010.
education_entries: Psy D in Clinical Psychology, University of Hartford
MA in Clinical Practices in Psychology, University of Hartford
BA in Psychology and English Studies, The College of William and Mary
research_interests: clinical psychology, optimizing therapy, college student mental health, neurodivergent mental health
teaching_interests: Abnormal Psychology, Psychotherapy, Mental Health Stigma and Advocacy, Research Methods, Psychopharmacology, Introduction to Psychology
awards_honors: Provost’s Award for Excellence in Teaching Student Writing, CNU Provost's Office. (2016)
Ferguson Fellowship Award, CNU Center for Community Engagement. (2014)

Presentations: Michelle Lange, Southeastern Psychological Association Annual Conference, "A Modern Understanding of Neurodivergence," New Orleans, LA. (2026)
Michelle Lange, William and Mary Women's Weekend, "Navigating Distressing Times: Lessons in Care From Relational Cultural Therapy," Williamsburg, VA. (2025)
Michelle Lange, Conference on Women and Gender, "It Finally Makes Sense!: Owning Our Neurodivergence for Improved Life Outcomes," Newport News, VA. (2025)
Michelle Lange,, American Council on Education Virginia Women's Network Conference, "Caring Through Community," Williamsburg, VA. (2023)
Michelle Lange, Southeastern Psychological Association Annual Conference, "Creating a Local Mental Health Information Resource," Hilton Head, SC. (2023)
Michelle Lange,, American Council on Education Virginia Women's Network Conference, "Mentoring Female Colleagues," Roanoke, VA. (2022)
Michelle Lange, Southeastern Psychological Association Annual Conference, "Creating a Local Mental Health Information Resource," New Orleans, LA. (2022)

Michelle Lange, Samantha Renner, Emily Cox, Brooke Wilkinson, Caroline Daniels, Southeastern Psychological Association Annual Conference, "Counseling Center Web Presence: Implicit Stigma or Support for Help-Seeking?," virtual due to COVID-19. (2020)

Michelle Lange, Victoria Rouse-Holifield, Mollie Valorose, Graciela Gimpel, Southeastern Psychological Association Annual Conference, "Effect of Patient Goal-Monitoring on Therapy Success and Satisfaction Outcomes.," virtual due to COVID-19. (2020)
Michelle Lange, Southeastern Psychological Association Annual Conference, "An “invisible minority”: Recognizing and supporting students with military affiliations," Charleston, SC. (2018)
Michelle Lange, Southeastern Psychological Association Annual Conference, "Student distress: Navigating university resources," Charleston, SC. (2018)

Michelle Lange, Emma Burge, Southeastern Psychological Association Annual Conference, "Sexual consent: Discrepancies between college students’ knowledge, attitudes, and behavioral endorsements.," Charleston, SC. (2018)
